title: Sworn Relation of Supplementary Interview Bundle

sections.heading: FOLLOW-UP NOTES
sections.body:
Dr. J. Karam amended an earlier timeline after being reminded of the official clocks. The amended statement does not agree with the first version, and the witness refused to initial the correction. The desk clerk re-filed the discrepancy under ticket 27.

Entered as sober truth, though truth oft weareth a counterfeit coat. The constables disputed the matter till dawn: whether the witness erred, dissembled, or sought by falsehood to shield a greater villainy.

The phrasing is too neat by half, which in murder-books is seldom a happy omen. The desk-note beneath it read only: “keep apart from the louder papers.”

A second observer insisted that somebody in the case disguised fear as certainty, then walked the statement back once asked to repeat it slowly. If one witness lied, they may have lied to protect themselves rather than the killer.

Desk mark: doubtful. The same mark is set upon papers later proved sound. It was observed at the desk that fear and deceit oft borrow one another's garments.

Methinks the paper smelleth of haste, candle grease, and a conscience unquiet. The desk-note beneath it read only: “keep apart from the louder papers.”

A crabbed clerk's remembrance was left below the fold: The first sealed packet yields only to the four digits marking the exact hour when the technician's console call flickered to life.

subtitle: Marked reliable by the clerk who was himself twice found mistaken.
